title: The Gettysburg Address
mainSkill: Key Ideas and Details, Craft and Structure, Integration of Knowledge and Ideas, Author's Purpose and Perspective

q.en: According to the book, when was the Gettysburg Address delivered?
q.zh: 根据本书，葛底斯堡演说是在什么时候发表的？

options.en: On April 9, 1865, when the Civil War finally came to an end
options.zh: 1865年4月9日，内战终于结束时

options.en: On July 1, 1863, at the very start of the Battle of Gettysburg according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 1863年7月1日，葛底斯堡战役刚刚打响时（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: On March 4, 1861, when Lincoln first took office as president
options.zh: 1861年3月4日，林肯刚就任总统时

options.en: On November 19, 1863, during the dedication of the Soldiers’ National Cemetery at Gettysburg
options.zh: 1863年11月19日，在葛底斯堡士兵国家公墓落成典礼期间

explain.en: The book states the cemetery was dedicated on November 19, 1863, and Lincoln gave his short remarks there.
explain.zh: 书中说公墓于1863年11月19日举行落成典礼，林肯在当天发表了简短讲话。

q.en: What does Lincoln mean by the phrase “four score and seven years ago”?
q.zh: 林肯所说的“八十七年前”是什么意思？

options.en: Seven years before the Founding Fathers wrote the Constitution
options.zh: 建国先贤撰写宪法之前的七年

options.en: A poetic way of saying the Civil War had already dragged on for four score and seven terrible, bloody, and devastating years
options.zh: 一种诗意的说法，意思是内战已经持续了八十七个可怕、血腥而毁灭性的年头

options.en: Eighty-seven years earlier, pointing back to 1776 and the Declaration of Independence
options.zh: 八十七年前，回溯到1776年《独立宣言》发表之时

options.en: Four hundred and seven years before the speech was given
options.zh: 演说发表之前四百零七年

explain.en: The book explains that four score and seven is eighty-seven years, taking the listener back to 1776, the year of the Declaration of Independence.
explain.zh: 书中解释说“四乘二十加七”等于八十七年，把听众带回到1776年，也就是《独立宣言》发表的那一年。

q.en: Who was the main speaker at the cemetery dedication ceremony?
q.zh: 在公墓落成典礼上，主要的演讲者是谁？

options.en: Edward Everett, a well-known speaker who gave a two-hour formal speech
options.zh: 爱德华·埃弗里特，一位知名演讲者，发表了长达两小时的正式演说

options.en: President Abraham Lincoln, who was the main speaker and gave the most famous short speech of the day
options.zh: 亚伯拉罕·林肯总统，他是当天的主要演讲者并发表了最著名的简短演说

options.en: Robert E. Lee, the leading general of the Confederate army
options.zh: 南方邦联军队的统帅罗伯特·E·李将军

options.en: Thomas Jefferson, the author of the Declaration of Independence
options.zh: 《独立宣言》的起草人托马斯·杰斐逊

explain.en: Lincoln was invited to give “a few appropriate remarks,” but the main speaker was Edward Everett, who spoke for two hours.
explain.zh: 林肯受邀“讲几句合适的话”，但主要演讲者是爱德华·埃弗里特，他讲了两个小时。

q.en: About how many men died in the American Civil War, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，大约有多少人在美国内战中丧生？

options.en: Around 3,500 Union soldiers were buried at the Gettysburg cemetery
options.zh: 约3500名联邦士兵安葬在葛底斯堡公墓

options.en: About 620,000 men lost their lives in the war
options.zh: 战争中约有62万人失去了生命

options.en: More than 620,000 men died between 1861 and 1865
options.zh: 1861至1865年间有超过62万人丧生

options.en: Almost 51,000 soldiers were killed in a single battle at Gettysburg
options.zh: 仅葛底斯堡一役就有近5.1万名士兵阵亡

explain.en: The book says that from 1861 until 1865 the Civil War raged and 620,000 men died in it.
explain.zh: 书中写道，从1861年到1865年内战激烈进行，共有62万人在战争中死去。

q.en: Which famous phrase closes the Gettysburg Address?
q.zh: 葛底斯堡演说以哪句著名的话结尾？

options.en: Four score and seven years ago our fathers brought forth a new nation according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 八十七年前，我们的先辈在这个大陆上建立了一个新国家（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: We are met on a great battlefield of that war
options.zh: 我们在这场战争的一个伟大战场上集会

options.en: Government of the people, by the people, for the people, shall not perish from the earth
options.zh: 民有、民治、民享的政府，必不至于从地球上消失

options.en: The world will little note, nor long remember what we say here
options.zh: 世人不会注意，也不会长久记住我们在此所说的话

explain.en: The speech ends with the line about government of the people, by the people, for the people, which Lincoln repeats with simple prepositions.
explain.zh: 演说以“民有、民治、民享的政府”一句作结，林肯用简单的介词变换重复强调了这一点。

q.en: Why does the author say we still read Lincoln’s speech today?
q.zh: 作者认为我们今天仍在阅读林肯演说的原因是什么？

options.en: Because its brevity and powerful message continue to inspire people
options.zh: 因为它的简洁和有力的信息至今仍在激励人们

options.en: Because it is the longest and most detailed speech ever delivered at any American cemetery
options.zh: 因为它是美国任何公墓落成典礼上最长、最详尽的演说

options.en: Because it was the only speech ever recorded with early sound technology
options.zh: 因为它是唯一用早期录音技术记录下来的演说

options.en: Because Lincoln was by far the most popular speaker of his time
options.zh: 因为林肯是他那个时代最受欢迎的演讲者

explain.en: The author says people revere the speech because Lincoln preserved a vision of a nation founded on equality, and his words inspire change.
explain.zh: 作者说人们敬重这篇演说，因为林肯保留了一个建立在平等之上的国家愿景，他的话语激励着变革。

q.en: How many handwritten copies of the Gettysburg Address exist?
q.zh: 《葛底斯堡演说》现存多少份手写稿？

options.en: Only one signed copy hangs in the White House Lincoln Room
options.zh: 白宫林肯厅里只挂着一份有签名的正本

options.en: Five copies written in Lincoln’s own hand, all slightly different
options.zh: 五份林肯亲笔写就的稿子，彼此略有不同

options.en: More than one hundred different printed newspaper versions appeared across the country
options.zh: 全国出现了一百多份不同的印刷报纸版本

options.en: Two copies, one kept by the North and one by the South
options.zh: 两份，北方和南方各保存一份

explain.en: The book notes that five copies written in Lincoln’s own hand exist, and all are slightly different from one another.
explain.zh: 书中提到现存五份林肯亲笔写就的稿子，彼此都略有不同。

q.en: What does the book say about Lincoln’s health after the speech?
q.zh: 关于演说之后林肯的健康状况，书中说了什么？

options.en: He felt completely healthy and gave another long speech the very next day according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他感觉完全健康，第二天又发表了一篇长篇演说（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He caught a small cold that lasted only a single quiet afternoon
options.zh: 他得了小感冒，只安静地持续了一个下午

options.en: He was a little tired but rode home on the train feeling perfectly fine
options.zh: 他有点累，但乘火车回家时感觉很好

options.en: He boarded the train home feverish with a severe headache and was later diagnosed with smallpox
options.zh: 他乘火车回家时发烧并剧烈头痛，后来被诊断出得了天花

explain.en: Witnesses describe Lincoln as sad and ill; by the time he boarded the train home he was feverish with a severe headache and was later diagnosed with smallpox.
explain.zh: 目击者描述林肯神情哀伤且身体不适；他上火车回家时已发烧并剧烈头痛，后来被诊断出天花。

q.en: How did the audience first react to Lincoln’s speech?
q.zh: 听众最初对林肯的演说作何反应？

options.en: The crowd roared with loud cheers and celebration immediately after he finished speaking
options.zh: 他刚讲完，人群立刻爆发出响亮的欢呼和庆祝

options.en: The reaction was quiet, with a hushed or delayed, scattered applause
options.zh: 现场很安静，只有一片肃穆或延迟、零星的掌声

options.en: Newspapers across the country printed it on the front page the very next morning
options.zh: 全国报纸第二天一早就把演说登在了头版

options.en: Everyone stood and cheered for a full two hours, just as they did for Everett
options.zh: 所有人都起立欢呼了两小时，就像为埃弗里特那样

explain.en: The book says the reaction was quiet, with delayed scattered applause or a dignified silence, not the loud response some might expect.
explain.zh: 书中说现场反应安静，只有延迟而零星的掌声或庄重的沉默，并非一些人预期的热烈回应。

q.en: In the speech, what do the words “hallow” and “consecrate” mean?
q.zh: 在演说中，“神圣化”和“祝圣”这两个词是什么意思？

options.en: To honor or make sacred, since the soldiers’ deaths already made the ground holy
options.zh: 表示尊崇或使之神圣，因为烈士的牺牲已使这片土地神圣

options.en: To forget the past and move on to a brand-new beginning
options.zh: 忘掉过去，迈向一个全新的开始

options.en: To build a new house or a large public monument for visiting tourists according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为游客新建一所房子或一座大型公共纪念碑（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To win a battle and completely defeat the enemy in war
options.zh: 在战争中打赢一场战斗并彻底击败敌人

explain.en: Lincoln uses hallow, meaning to honor as holy, and consecrate, meaning to make sacred, saying the soldiers already made the ground sacred.
explain.zh: 林肯用“hallow”（尊为神圣）和“consecrate”（使之圣洁）表示，烈士们的牺牲已让这片土地无比神圣。

prompt.en: Imagine you are at the Gettysburg cemetery in 1863. Write two or three sentences explaining why the soldiers who fought there should be remembered.
prompt.zh: 设想你身处1863年的葛底斯堡公墓。写两三句话，说明为何应当铭记在那里战斗的士兵。

en: Think about the word “sacrifice” and how the book describes the soldiers giving their lives
Use the idea that the soldiers made the ground sacred through their actions
Keep your sentences short and clear, like Lincoln’s own brief style
zh: 想想“牺牲”一词，以及书中如何描述士兵献出生命
运用“士兵以行动使这片土地神圣”这一想法
像林肯简洁的风格一样，把句子写得简短清晰
